judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly equivalent, but Report 1 additionally identifies the missing env/deploy-level global gate (expectation d), making it slightly more complete.

report_1.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... when `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` is `None` (key never set...), the function returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... if the key is absent from `tenant.settings` ... `tenant.settings.get(...)` returns `None`, the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` so a `None`/missing value returns `False` instead of `True`

report_2.evidence: I'd change the default at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting it to default to `False`

report_1.evidence: `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") first introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the `is None: return True` pattern, for a UI display preference ... the two flags have different blast radii and should not share a default.

report_2.evidence: a copy-paste of the "default missing setting to `True`" pattern that `compact_calendar_view_enabled` established ... `compact_calendar_view_enabled` gates a harmless cosmetic UI preference ... the two settings have completely different risk profiles.

report_1.evidence: **Add a rollback/kill-switch**: there is currently no env-level master switch for this feature anywhere in the repo (confirmed via grep) — only the per-tenant DB value gates it ... a rollout like this should combine the tenant value with an env-level master switch (ANDed together)
